rationale: Ground truth shows a trivial, cleanly-approved rename PR with zero substantive concerns, so recall is trivially satisfied by both. X's verification-heavy APPROVE (grep for leftovers, upstream cross-check) matches the actual reviewer signal exactly with no fabricated asks. Y is also well-grounded (real file/line, verified via grep) but manufactures two minor scope-creep nitpicks (untested pre-existing fallback path, a suggested comment) that push the verdict to COMMENT, diverging from the human's plain lgtm — technically valid but of marginal necessity, lowering precision even though the concrete file/line detail makes it more actionable.
